verses.text: Job's heart cries, 'oh that I knew where I might find him!'; he would lay his case before God and fill his mouth with arguments. He longs not to flee God but to reach Him. The deepest cry of the afflicted soul is to come near to God Himself.

Job's heart cries, 'oh that I knew where I might find him!'; he would lay his case before God and fill his mouth with arguments. He longs not to flee God but to reach Him. The deepest cry of the afflicted soul is to come near to God Himself.

Job's heart cries, 'oh that I knew where I might find him!'; he would lay his case before God and fill his mouth with arguments. He longs not to flee God but to reach Him. The deepest cry of the afflicted soul is to come near to God Himself.

Job's heart cries, 'oh that I knew where I might find him!'; he would lay his case before God and fill his mouth with arguments. He longs not to flee God but to reach Him. The deepest cry of the afflicted soul is to come near to God Himself.

Would God overwhelm him with power? No, Job believes He would give him a hearing — yet search as he may, he cannot find Him on any side. God seems hidden. The believer may seek and not perceive Him, and still trust that He is near.

Would God overwhelm him with power? No, Job believes He would give him a hearing — yet search as he may, he cannot find Him on any side. God seems hidden. The believer may seek and not perceive Him, and still trust that He is near.

Then faith shines: 'he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold.' Job has kept God's way and prized His words above his food. Trial is the furnace; the tested believer emerges purer, not consumed.

Then faith shines: 'he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold.' Job has kept God's way and prized His words above his food. Trial is the furnace; the tested believer emerges purer, not consumed.

Then faith shines: 'he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold.' Job has kept God's way and prized His words above his food. Trial is the furnace; the tested believer emerges purer, not consumed.

God is unchanging in purpose and 'performeth the thing that is appointed' for Job. He bows to a settled, sovereign will. There is rest in knowing our trials are not random but appointed by a wise and faithful God.

God is unchanging in purpose and 'performeth the thing that is appointed' for Job. He bows to a settled, sovereign will. There is rest in knowing our trials are not random but appointed by a wise and faithful God.

Yet that very thought makes Job tremble, his heart melting before God's presence. Awe and dread mingle in him. To stand before the living God is fearful even to the faithful, who feel their smallness in His light.
